title: Clarify Antigravity startup behavior and integration path
description: Holistic currently documents the desired Antigravity startup contract, but one-time repo initialization does not itself guarantee that Antigravity IDE will ask the user what to do on repo open. Preserve this gap as follow-up work so the next session can either tighten the docs or implement a real Antigravity startup integration if one exists.
acceptance_criteria: Docs no longer imply one-time init alone guarantees Antigravity auto-resume; or a real Antigravity startup hook/workspace automation is identified and integrated.

title: Andon service auto-start (API + dashboard) on repo open
description: When a developer opens the Holistic repo, the Andon API (port 4318) and dashboard dev server (port 5173) should start automatically in the background. Currently requires manual startup. Auto-start should be idempotent (skip if already running). Wired via Claude Code SessionStart hook calling .holistic-local/system/start-andon.ps1.

title: Test: killTree terminates Andon child processes on daemon shutdown
description: Integration test verifying that when the daemon stops (SIGTERM/SIGINT), killTree is called on Andon child processes and those processes are actually dead afterward. On Windows this means taskkill /t /f cleans the full process tree including vite grandchildren. Requires spawning real child processes in the test.

title: Bake holistic_handoff call into every adapter file as explicit session-end instruction
description: Every adapter file should include an explicit end-of-session instruction: 'before ending this conversation, call holistic_handoff'. Currently a separate user habit, not baked in. Write-back reliability for non-hook paths depends on this.
notes:
## Implementation slices

**Files:** src/core/docs.ts (renderAdapter, renderRootAgentDoc, renderAgentsMd, renderHolisticMd)

### Slice 1 — Define standard session-close block
- Write a shared renderSessionCloseBlock(hasMcp: boolean) helper
- MCP-capable agents: 'Before ending this session, call holistic_handoff with a summary of what you did and what should happen next.'
- Non-MCP agents (Codex, Cursor, etc.): 'Before ending this session, run: holistic handoff --summary "..." --next "..."'

### Slice 2 — Inject into renderAdapter() 
- Add session-close block to the bottom of every adapter doc (docs.ts lines 427-470)
- Applies to: codex.md, claude-cowork.md, antigravity.md and the new cursorrules/windsurf/copilot files from holistic-7i6

### Slice 3 — Inject into renderRootAgentDoc()
- CLAUDE.md and GEMINI.md should also carry the instruction

### Slice 4 — Inject into renderAgentsMd() / renderHolisticMd()
- AGENTS.md contributor workflow section should include it
- HOLISTIC.md contributor workflow section should include it

### Slice 5 — Verify no duplication on re-runs
- writeDerivedDocs() regenerates from templates — no state tracking needed, just ensure template always includes it

title: Test: startAndonServices spawns API and dashboard on free ports
description: Unit/integration test verifying that startAndonServices spawns the Andon API process when port 4318 is free and the dashboard process when port 5173 is free, and skips both when ports are already occupied. Requires process lifecycle management in the test (spawn + kill). See daemon.ts startAndonServices().

title: Fix stuck Fleet objective and parked status drift in Andon live board
description: Mission Control repeatedly shows the same stale card as active context: "cursor · Parked · Implementing M007: Event Forwarding (Daemon & Wrappers)" even after new work, restarts, and refresh. Live board behavior is still being influenced by stale legacy/session narrative events or stale activeSession carryover instead of authoritative runtime truth. This erodes operator trust in Andon's real-time supervision role.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Fleet live status/objective comes from runtime harness truth (runtime_sessions + runtime_processes + runtime_events) only. 2) A stale legacy objective cannot remain pinned after runtime status changes. 3) "needs_input" and "flowing" states require current runtime evidence (waiting/running + fresh heartbeat), not historical question/resume markers. 4) If runtime feed is unavailable, board shows explicit disconnected/degraded runtime state instead of stale inferred activity. 5) Regression tests cover the stuck-objective scenario and prevent recurrence.
close_reason: resolved via merged PR #5 (runtime-truth boundary + CI stabilization)

title: Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook — periodic write-back for uncommitted work
description: Install a Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook that periodically snapshots state for non-commit work. Closes the write-back dead zone: planning, research, architectural decisions that never produce a commit currently vanish.
notes:
## Implementation slices

**Files:** src/core/setup.ts (extend installClaudeCodeHooks from holistic-q3f)

**Dependency:** holistic-q3f must land first (establishes .claude/settings.json merge pattern)

### Slice 1 — Design the debounce strategy
- UserPromptSubmit fires on every prompt — calling checkpoint every time is too noisy
- Approach: write a lightweight wrapper script that checks last-checkpoint timestamp, only checkpoints if >15 min elapsed
- Store last-auto-checkpoint time in .holistic/state.json

### Slice 2 — Add addLastAutoCheckpoint field to HolisticState (types.ts)
- lastAutoCheckpoint: ISO timestamp string | undefined

### Slice 3 — Write the debounce shell script (generated in .holistic/system/)
- check-and-checkpoint.sh / check-and-checkpoint.ps1
- Reads lastAutoCheckpoint from state.json, compares to now, calls holistic checkpoint if threshold exceeded
- Reason string: 'auto periodic snapshot'

### Slice 4 — Extend installClaudeCodeHooks() to add UserPromptSubmit entry
- Add to .claude/settings.json hooks block alongside SessionStart
- Command: call check-and-checkpoint script

### Slice 5 — Smoke test
- Verify hook fires, respects debounce, and only checkpoints when threshold exceeded
